prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr! Listen well, ye scallywag, and I’ll spin ye the truth o’ the blue heavens above! ‘Tis not the work o’ Neptune nor the tears o’ mermaids, though many a lubber swears it so. Nay—the sky be blue ‘cause o’ the very air we breathe, ye see!

When the sun’s golden glare cuts through the sky like a cutlass through fog, its light be scramblin’ with the wee particles and vapors floatin’ in the breeze. Now, that sunlight be made o’ all the colors o’ the rainbow—but blue light, bein’ feisty and short-winded like a bilge rat in a squall, scatters quicker and wider than the rest! So when ye cast yer gaze upward, ‘tis mostly that blue mischief ye behold, dancin’ and scatterin’ all ‘cross the dome o’ heaven!

So next time ye squint at the sky, remember: ‘tis no magic, but the sea’s own cousin—light itself—playin’ tricks with the air! Savvy? Now pass the grog—I’ve earned me a swig for such salty wisdom!
